title: Publish via a transactional outbox
description: Write the event to an outbox table in the same transaction as the state change, then relay it to the broker separately.
body: Use when a state change must reliably produce an event (order placed -> OrderPlaced). Do not write to the database and then publish to the broker as two independent steps (a dual write that can half-fail). Actions: insert the event row into an outbox table inside the same DB transaction as the business write; a separate relay (poller or CDC) reads unpublished rows, publishes, and marks them sent at-least-once. Validation signals include a test that a committed write always yields exactly one eventually-published event and a rolled-back write yields none. Risk boundary: a dual write with no outbox can silently lose or fabricate events and is a blocking correctness finding.
